# SignalFlow Studio Full Context

SignalFlow Studio is an open-source, local-first autoposting workspace. The user gives information once, captures or adds visual media when useful, selects social/output channels, and receives a formatted posting package. The package can include copy, generated visual cards, screenshot/recording/GIF plans, model prompts, Markdown exports, JSON summaries, and safe publishing handoff metadata.

## Product Positioning

SignalFlow Studio is not only a caption generator. It is intended to reduce the manual work involved in preparing social posts: writing copy, deciding what format each platform needs, capturing screenshots, recording screens, turning media into GIF/video ideas, and preparing assets for review.

## Audience

- Solo creators
- Founders
- Indie hackers
- Developers
- Open-source maintainers
- DevRel teams
- Product builders
- Small marketing teams

## Problem

Users often need to post product updates, demos, launches, changelogs, tutorials, and technical progress across multiple platforms. Doing this manually requires:

- Writing platform-specific copy
- Taking screenshots
- Recording product flows
- Creating GIFs or short videos
- Deciding post structure
- Reformatting for every channel
- Copying assets between tools
- Reviewing before posting

SignalFlow Studio aims to reduce that into one main action: provide information, then generate a complete reviewable package.

## Outputs

- LinkedIn post drafts
- X post drafts
- Instagram caption starters
- Blog drafts
- Newsletter drafts
- Release notes
- Generated image/card asset
- Visual media plan
- Screen recording and screenshot capture entries
- Model prompt for local SLM/API/chatbot use
- Markdown export
- JSON export
- Integration metadata

## Current Architecture

- Frontend: Next.js App Router UI.
- API routes: Next.js app routes inside the hosted product.
- Core package: `signalflow/` contains ingestion, scoring, image rendering, context generation, and launch/post kit logic.
- Local-first model: users can keep data local and route prompts to local SLMs, API models, cloud gateways, or copy-paste chatbot workflows.

## Safety Principles

SignalFlow Studio should use manual review, official APIs, exports, or webhooks for distribution. It should not use stealth platform clients, private GraphQL clients, credential scraping, browser-session scraping, or publishing without explicit approval.
